metric_value: Database must scale in users, database size, and performance; Oracle8 targets all three
notes: First of three PSK evaluation framework pillars

metric_value: Database must deliver support for rapid change via relational and object-relational data models
Database must cut deployment/administration/maintenance costs; Oracle8 targets with partitioning and automated backup
Oracle8 reduces per-user memory requirements by approximately 50% vs Oracle7 via pooled and multiplexed connections
Oracle8 supports more than 10,000 end users per node; dramatic increase over Oracle7
Oracle8 improves performance on 2.7-million row star query by order of magnitude over Oracle 7.3, two orders of magnitude over Oracle 7.0
notes: Oracle internal benchmark cited by PSK

metric_value: Oracle8 parallelized replication delivers speeds an order of magnitude faster in tests
Oracle8 underwent 10-month beta test with nearly 100 partners and over 1,000 customers
notes: Beta program scale indicates enterprise validation

metric_value: Upgrade from Oracle7 to Oracle8 is relatively fast and simple; existing applications work without change
Object-relational value lies in increased developer productivity via richer data model, not primarily in multimedia
notes: PSK distinguishes OR value from multimedia hype

metric_value: Oracle8 extensibility mechanism allows ISV/IS-created data cartridges to access core RDBMS functions including query optimizer and indexing
Oracle8 Advanced Queuing places messaging in the database engine, reducing middleware complexity and improving performance
Oracle8 table partitioning allows dividing tables into smaller chunks for manageability, performance, and reduced downtime
Oracle8 adds support for images and time-series data beyond Oracle7's text/spatial/video; plus extensibility for custom types
Bristol Myers cited as large-scale real-world customer using Oracle7 multimedia capabilities
Pacific Bell cited as large-scale real-world customer using Oracle7 spatial data capabilities
Oracle will add further object-relational features, further integrate relational and object data, and improve object-access performance over next 1-2 years
Oracle8i (1998) delivered Java VM integration, XML support, and further OR enhancements; Oracle9i (2001) added RAC and XML DB; predictions proved substantially correct
notes: PSK prediction confirmed by Oracle8i and Oracle9i releases

metric_value: Aberdeen/Kastner concludes Oracle8 is 'powerful and profound' — sets new standard for handling exceptionally broad range of customer needs
Oracle NCA incorporates major standards including Java and CORBA with open interfaces
Java access to Oracle8 object data to be supported in Oracle 8.1
notes: Oracle8.1 = Oracle8i released 1998; Java VM confirmed

metric_value: Oracle8 can support hundreds of terabytes of data
